data.subject: information request - DB

emails.body:
Hi Kathy/Tom,

I'm not sure if you are the correct person to ask this, but do either of you maintain the renewals tracker? If so, can you please send the most up-to-date version of this file? This would be required to get a sense of how much of our revenues is contracted vs on a month to month basis.

@Jorge Quintero do we have a report that can provide remaining term per Service ID#?

Thanks,

Paul Billard

Financial Analyst

aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Dennis Lam <Dennis.Lam@aptum.com>
Sent: March 16, 2026 4:55 PM
To: Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>; Paul Billard <Paul.Billard@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; George Revie <George.Revie@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: information request - DB

HI Sarah,

For contribution margin, I only have the high level general margins by LOB.

If DB wants it per customer's MRR, I think utilizing the profitability data Jorge put together will be best. And contribution margin would be Revenues less any direct costs associated with customer, excluding rent and other overheads that isnt captured by Jorge.

Dennis Lam

Manager, Finance

+1 (604) 909-6386
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>
Sent: Monday, March 16, 2026 1:44 PM
To: Dennis Lam <Dennis.Lam@aptum.com>; Paul Billard <Paul.Billard@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>;

Adam Nelson

Senior Director, Service Delivery

+1 (438) 802-9796
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Sent: March 16, 2026 17:26

Subject: Re: information request - DB

Hi Sarah,

Sales Pipeline data is attached with a view of total open MRC, NRC, and TCV for the remainder of FY26.

Figures are shown by fiscal quarter/month, and the table can be expanded to view the opps with detail on LOB/Type/Stage etc.

Please let me know if you would like to see the data in a different format.

Thanks,

Tom Kelsch

Senior Decision Support Analyst

aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>
Sent: Monday, March 16, 2026 1:44 PM
To: Dennis Lam <Dennis.Lam@aptum.com>; Paul Billard <Paul.Billard@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; George Revie <George.Revie@aptum.com>
Subject: information request - DB

Hey Team,

We do have a request coming in for updated information for Digital Bridge. For each of the below please indicate

Hi Sarah,

Please find attached requested information. Please note the following:

Top 10 Customer MRR details includes the details for the top 10 customers as of Feb 2026. MRR tab includes MRR amount for each customer by LOB for period starting Sep 2024 to Feb 2026. I've also included several details in contact info tab.

Backlog & Sales Tracker data file includes data I've leveraged from the Sales Tracker (Sep 2021 to Feb 2026) and MRR files to gather details regarding backlog and remaining contracted revenue deals as % of MRR.

Backlog:

Backlog includes amounts for MRC ($46M). This was gathered based on remaining term for each contract that extends beyond Feb 2026.

Backlog for NRC (excluding PS) is ($141K). This was based on completions of NRC deals.

For the PS backlog I've used the data from PS revenue sheet ($431K). This would be the remaining amounts unrecognized from the sheet.

% customer revenue on contract vs MRR:

While leveraging the sales results file and looking up contracted amounts that have remaining term, this shows these orders consist of $3.1M of our $5.8M MRR for Feb 2026. This would constitute 54% of our MRR. If 54% of our MRC is still under contract, would that mean that remaining 46% of MRR would have services which are on a Month to Month term?

The total % of MRR for these customers would constitute 67% of our MRR, but some of the services for these customers may have come out of contract.

The average contract term for these deals (excludes HWM,

Hi Sarah, attached is the CAPEX forecast as requested, I am still working on the Pro Forma for the lease renewals (Dennis is assisting).

George Revie

Director, Data Center Infrastructure & Operations

+1 (647) 826-3048
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Sent: Monday, March 16, 2026 4:44 PM

data.subject: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification

emails.body:
What should be our recommended approach here?

It seems we either need to:

Tell the customer it can't continue, in which case they will churn (they have expressed the stance to keep the same licence - "it's always been that way")

Stay as is and hope for the best.

I don't think that the second option is an option.

Frederic Gingras

Senior Director, Account Management

m: +1 514-913-9153 teams: +1 514-673-7553
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>
Sent: March 16, 2026 9:20 AM
To: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Elizabeth Perez <Elizabeth.Perez@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Charles Rutledge <Charles.Rutledge@aptum.com>
Cc: Information Security <information_security@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification

When the previous manager of Abuse (Joseph) was exited last year, a lot of historical knowledge was lost. It's also worth clarifying that he was a 1-man team; he had no reports. His Abuse responsibilities were given to InfoSec to handle as best we can, and since then, we have been trying to document some of that knowledge based on our interpretation of what Abuse should handle, which does align with what Adam said: the mis-use of our services.

I don't believe license (mis-)use should fall under the purview of Abuse. If a customer has in

All,

I want to close the loop on this. This ticket has been open for nearly a year, it's been passed around between multiple people (several of whom are no longer here).

A few things I want to clarify up front:

This is not an Abuse issue. Abuse handles misuse of our services illegal activity like bitcoin mining. This is a commercial licensing gap. Fred TY, your team can stand down on this one. That said, we do need to clearly define what routes to Abuse vs. what's a commercial matter so we don't repeat this.

Someone needs to own this. @Elizabeth Perez since you're already in communication with the customer, can you take the lead? Frederic and Barbara, please support as needed from the commercial and SDM side.

The economics are what they are. Smart Scale pays us roughly $2,500/month. The compliant licensing would cost them ~$7,900/month on top of that. They're not going to pay that. Threatening suspension hasn't worked for a year and it's not going to start working now.

We are currently liable and at risk as an organization if we do nothing!

Here's what I think we should do:

@Elizabeth Perez, schedule a call with Mario (let's stop the back and forth email chains). He clearly doesn't understand (or doesn't want to understand) the issue, and a year of back-and-forth hasn't helped. I feel like we should present the following options:

They can purchase per-core licenses through us (the expensive path, don't lead with it, but it is an option)

They can procure Software Ass

data.subject: Meet the Frontier Suite: Microsoft 365 E7 and Agent 365 are here

emails.body:
Plus: Explore the latest renewal resources, incentives, and skilling.

Having trouble viewing this email? | View as a web page

Introducing Microsoft 365 E7—the productivity suite for Frontier Transformation

We’re equipping partners and customers with new capabilities to advance Frontier Transformation—grounded in intelligence and trust.

Read more

On March 9, we introduced the Frontier Suite called Microsoft 365 E7, with general availability of Microsoft 365 E7 and Agent 365—the control plane for agents that enables partners and customers to manage and secure agents with confidence.

Microsoft 365 E7 is the productivity suite for a human‑led, agent‑operated enterprise, turning human intent into action with AI that runs securely at scale. Microsoft 365 E7 brings together Microsoft 365 E5, Microsoft 365 Copilot, and built-in governance through Agent 365—creating a powerful opportunity for partners to drive successful AI deployments and adoption.

During the month of March, there will be several opportunities to gain insight into this new announcement. You are encouraged to participate in the Agent 365 "Ask Microsoft Anything" session on March 18 and to register for the Digital Partner Airlift on March 31 to further your understanding of the Agentic System of Work.

To accelerate your Microsoft 365 E5 to E7 upsell and Agent 365 purchase conversations, we've made two updates to further support customer engagements through Copilot + Power Accelerate starting April 1:

Agent 365

Plus: Explore the latest renewal resources, incentives, and skilling.

Microsoft 365 E7 is the productivity suite for a human‑led, agent‑operated enterprise, turning human intent into action with AI that runs securely at scale. Microsoft 365 E7 brings together Microsoft 365 E5, Microsoft 365 Copilot, and built-in governance through Agent 365—creating a powerful opportunity for partners to drive successful AI deployments and adoption.

data.subject: Microsoft PSfP Premier Partner Forum - April 2026 - M365 E7

emails.body:
Hello Team,

Please join us Wednesday, April 1st at 11am EST for April's Premier Partner forum. This call will be a call around M365 E7 with a Principal Partner Solution Architect.

Please prepare any questions you may have in advance for our team!

Who Should Attend?

Business Development Managers

Technical Certification Specialists

Partner Admins

Account Managers

Engineering Leads

A one-time registration is required at the following link: Microsoft Virtual Events Powered by Teams

Attached you will find the meeting invite - please let me know if you have any questions.

M365 E7 Resources:

Introducing Microsoft 365 E7: The Frontier Suite

M365 E7 Launch Kit

M365 E7 CSP Pricelist (Available April 1st)

M365 E7 Prom Readiness Guide (Available April 1st)

Microsoft 365 Blog | Latest Product Updates and Insights

We hope to see you there!

Thank you,

data.subject: Your CRM Is Holding Back Your Revenue

emails.body:
Website →

Most CRMs were built to store contacts, not to create revenue.

That’s why so many teams find themselves exporting data, juggling multiple tools, and spending hours trying to turn static

contact lists into real conversations.

Your CRM becomes a database — not a growth engine.

Contact Sales

Contact Science changes that.

Instead of forcing your team to manage contacts manually, Contact Science transforms your prospecting and marketing into a data-driven system designed to create opportunities automatically.

With Contact Science, you can:

Discover and identify high-value prospects with precision

Launch targeted outreach campaigns in minutes

Track engagement and intent signals in real time

Automatically prioritize the prospects most likely to convert

Turn marketing data directly into qualified pipeline

Just clean data, intelligent targeting, and faster

pipeline growth.

Smarter Prospecting

Identify and prioritize the right accounts automatically

Build custom campaigns that cut through the noise, utilize multi-touch outreach

Equip sales with actionable insights, not just contact records

Give managers real-time visibility into every conversation and conversion

Learn more

Marketing That Actually Converts

Customizable templates to align campaigns to target market

Improve segmentation using real engagement signals

Close the loop between outreach, engagement, and revenue

Custom workflows, automated campaigns including Post Card mailings, and personaliz

emails.body:

Can you please send the US APN? I just looked on confluence for 10 mins through the 100 pages of AWS "documentation" and couldn't find it.

I give up.

Adam Nelson

Senior Director, Service Delivery

+1 (438) 802-9796
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Sent: March 16, 2026 15:27
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>; Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>
Cc: Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: AWS Cloud Marketplace

@Lacie Allen-Morley, @Erik Tanguay, @Andrei Ianouchkevitch

We are planning a transition of US (not any other regions right now) customers from Ingram to TD Synnex.

@Andrei Ianouchkevitch, your customers will be the ones that are most impacted (because it's migrating their billing and taking away their—if they even use it—access to cloudcheckr).

@Lacie Allen-Morley, we will need someone from your team (maybe Kristina?) to lead the X's and O's on this because we need to submit documents for EACH customer in the US region to get them migrated off Ingram.

& @Erik Tanguay - You're probably going to need to be involved from a billing perspective at the beginning/end of the month.
